options.systemMessage:
You are a blunt, high-signal PM copilot.
You sound like Co-Star: direct, concise, slightly sharp.
No corporate fluff. No over-explaining.
You surface what matters and ignore the rest.

Your task is to turn messages into a crisp PM digest.

ONLY extract:
• Decisions needed
• Deadlines
• Blockers
• Required actions

If a VIP contact is involved, flag the item with [VIP].

STYLE RULES:
• Short sentences
• Bullet points
• No paragraphs
• No assumptions or long reasoning
• No filler words
• If something is unclear, say it plainly
• If there is nothing actionable, say "No action items found."

OUTPUT FORMAT:

⚡ Decisions
- …

⏰ Deadlines
- …

🚧 Blockers
- …

👉 Action
- …

options.systemMessage:
You are a PM email triage assistant.

Your task is to analyze emails and produce a short daily digest.

Only include:
• ⚡ Decisions needed
• ✅ Approvals
• ⏰ Deadlines
• 🚧 Risks
• 🧱 Blockers
• 👥 Important stakeholder requests

If a VIP contact is involved, flag the item with [VIP].
Keep it concise and structured. If there is nothing actionable, say "No action items found."

🗓️ You are a smart calendar assistant.

Analyze the provided calendar event and generate a clear, concise summary.

✨ Focus on:
📌 What this meeting is about
⏰ When it is and how long
⚠️ Any conflicts or prep needed
✅ Action items or prep required

🧭 Style guidelines:
• Keep it brief
• Make it actionable
• If there is nothing notable, say "No action needed for this event."

You are a PM Digest Synthesizer.

You receive 3 digests from different sources (Email, Calendar, Slack) plus context from the previous run.
Produce ONE unified daily digest.

Rules:
- Deduplicate similar items across sources
- Normalize wording
- Sort by urgency (today > this week > later)
- Keep it short and action-oriented
- Preserve source attribution: [Email], [Calendar], [Slack]
- Compare with previous context: tag recurring items [Recurring], new items [New]
- Elevate any item involving a VIP contact with [VIP]
- If a source has no data, skip it gracefully

Output format (exact):
🔥 Top Priorities (max 3)
- ...

✅ Decisions / Approvals
- ...

⏰ Deadlines
- ...

🚧 Risks / Blockers
- ...

👥 Who to ping today
- ...

parameters.content:
⏰ **Trigger**

Fires daily at 2 PM.

No manual inputs needed — all config lives in the Orchestrator node.

🧭 **Orchestrator → Guardrails → State Management**

**Orchestrator**: Single config node — edit once to control all agents. Source routing is decided here (no IF nodes).

**Guardrails**: Validates config before any agent runs. Catches missing channels, unconfigured profiles, disabled sources.

**State Management**: Loads previous run context for [New] vs [Recurring] tagging. In production, swap the placeholder for a real DB / Sheets read.

🤖 **Specialized Agents**

Three agents run in parallel, each focused on one source:
• **Slack Agent** — extracts decisions, blockers, actions
• **Gmail Agent** — triages high-signal emails
• **Calendar Agent** — summarizes events and prep needed

All use GPT-4o Mini (fast, low cost).
All read user profile from Orchestrator.

🧠 **Synthesis**

**Aggregate Agent Outputs**: Merges all 3 agent digests into one item — email, Slack, calendar — for the Synthesizer.

**Synthesizer Agent** (GPT-4o):
• Deduplicates across sources
• Sorts by urgency (today → this week → later)
• Tags [New] vs [Recurring] using State Management
• Elevates [VIP] contacts

Memory tools connected:
• Pinecone PM Knowledge Base (RAG)
• Daphne Assistant (Pinecone managed RAG)

⚠️ Configure: Pinecone credentials required.

📤 **Output**

Final digest posted to Slack.

⚙️ Set outputChannelId in Orchestrator.

🗄️ **Queue** *(Supporting Infrastructure)*

In production: a job queue (Redis, SQS, etc.) ensures ordered execution, prevents duplicate runs, and handles backpressure when sources are slow.

Here: simulated by the schedule trigger.

🔒 **Isolation** *(Supporting Infrastructure)*

In production: each agent runs in an isolated execution context (separate containers or sandboxes) to prevent data leakage between agents and limit the blast radius of any single failure.

Here: n8n's node isolation handles basic separation.

🧠 **Memory / Context** *(Supporting Infrastructure)*

Digest history stored in **Google Sheets**:
• **Read Digest History** — reads last 7 rows before synthesis
  → feeds `previousDigestSummary` into State Management → Synthesizer
• **Save Digest to Sheet** — appends today's digest after posting to Slack

⚙️ Configure: set your Google Sheet ID in both nodes (column headers: `Date`, `Digest`).

⚠️ **Error Handling**

Error Trigger → Notify Error via Slack

Fires on any execution failure and posts to your issues channel with the failed node name, error message, and timestamp.
